cell_lines.name: SH-SY5Y
cell_lines.disease_relevance: Differentiates into dopaminergic/cholinergic neuron-like cells. Widely used for neurotoxicity, amyloid-beta, and tau studies.

cell_lines.name: SH-SY5Y
cell_lines.disease_relevance: Standard model for dopaminergic neuron studies. Differentiate with retinoic acid for mature neuronal phenotype.

cell_lines.name: LUHMES
cell_lines.disease_relevance: Conditionally immortalized human dopaminergic neurons. More physiologically relevant than SH-SY5Y for PD.

cell_lines.name: INS-1
cell_lines.disease_relevance: Glucose-responsive insulin-secreting beta cell line. Gold standard for insulin secretion studies.

cell_lines.name: HepG2
cell_lines.disease_relevance: Hepatocyte model for glucose metabolism, insulin signaling, lipid metabolism, and PPAR-gamma activity.

cell_lines.name: HeLa
cell_lines.disease_relevance: Universal cancer cell line. Well-characterized for cytotoxicity screening, apoptosis, and cell cycle studies.

cell_lines.name: MCF7
cell_lines.disease_relevance: ER-positive breast cancer. Standard model for anti-estrogen drug screening and hormone-responsive cancer studies.

cell_lines.name: HUVEC
cell_lines.disease_relevance: Primary endothelial cells for angiogenesis, endothelial dysfunction, atherosclerosis, and vascular inflammation studies.

cell_lines.name: H9c2
cell_lines.disease_relevance: Cardiomyoblast line. Differentiates into cardiomyocyte-like cells. Used for cardiotoxicity, ischemia-reperfusion, and cardioprotection studies.

cell_lines.name: THP-1
cell_lines.disease_relevance: Monocytic cell line. Differentiate with PMA to macrophages. Standard model for inflammation, cytokine release, and immune response.

cell_lines.name: Jurkat
cell_lines.disease_relevance: T-cell lymphoma. Standard for T-cell activation, signaling, and immunomodulatory compound screening.
